pattern: Choosing which of the n-1 adjacent gaps are the equal ones is a binomial coefficient, and once that choice is fixed the array is built left to right with one free choice for the first value and m-1 free choices at every gap forced to differ.
hints: The n-1 gaps between consecutive elements split into exactly k that must match and the rest that must differ; first count how many ways to pick which k gaps those are.

Given that choice, build the array left to right: the very first element has m possibilities, and every later element is forced (matching gap) or has m-1 choices (differing gap) independent of the actual value chosen before it.

The total is C(n-1, k) * m * (m-1)^(n-1-k) taken modulo 1e9+7, using precomputed factorials for the binomial coefficient and fast modular exponentiation for the power (treating (m-1)^0 as 1 even when m=1).

edge_inputs.description: single-element array has no gaps at all
only one value available, all gaps forced to match
only one value available but a differing gap is impossible
two-length array, gap forced to match
two-length array, gap forced to differ
every gap must match, largest single-value count

stress.description: random n, m, k respecting 0 <= k <= n-1
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 100000)))
    m = rng.randint(1, 100000)
    k = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if n > 1 else 0
    return [n, m, k]
